summary: Small closed/unmerged interface cleanup in the player module: changed Player_GetControllerIndex's return type from signed 8-bit to unsigned 8-bit in both src/melee/pl/player.c and src/melee/pl/player.h. The body still declares a local s8 controller_index in the diff excerpt, so the visible change is specifically the public function signature/API type, likely aligning controller indices with unsigned slot/index semantics.
what_changed: Updated Player_GetControllerIndex(s32 slot) return type in player.c from s8 to u8.
Updated the matching declaration in player.h from s8 Player_GetControllerIndex(s32 slot) to u8 Player_GetControllerIndex(s32 slot).
No review discussion was present in the provided PR slice; the PR was closed without a recorded merge timestamp.
smart_moves: Kept the implementation and header declaration synchronized, avoiding a prototype/definition mismatch for Player_GetControllerIndex.
Made a narrow two-line type correction rather than mixing unrelated player-interface changes into the patch.
Used u8 for a controller index return value, which better communicates non-negative index-like data than s8, based on the visible signature change.
decomp_lessons: For interface cleanup, always update both the C definition and the header prototype together; even one-byte signedness changes are part of the public ABI/source contract.

Controller indices and similar slot/index values should be checked for signedness assumptions during decomp work; names that imply indices often warrant unsigned types, but local temporaries and struct fields should be verified before broad propagation.
Small header corrections can improve downstream type propagation in decompiled code, but evidence should come from matching context, callsites, or struct fields before changing more types.
naming_conventions: Player module functions use the Player_ prefix with PascalCase-style verbs, e.g. Player_GetControllerIndex, Player_SetCostumeId, Player_CheckSlot.
Parameter names in this interface use snake_case for semantic values, e.g. costume_id and controller_index.
Player slot arguments are represented as s32 slot in the visible interface.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No explicit assembly or matching tactic was documented in the PR slice.

Type-only signature fixes like s8-to-u8 can affect generated casts/sign-extension at call boundaries; future work should verify whether unsigned return typing improves matches before expanding similar changes.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided context.
Because the PR was closed with merged_at null, there is no evidence in the slice that this exact change landed via this PR.
follow_up_queries: Find later commits or PRs that changed Player_GetControllerIndex to confirm whether the u8 return type eventually landed.
Inspect StaticPlayer.controller_index field type and callsites of Player_GetControllerIndex to verify whether u8 is consistently correct.
Search for other Player_Get*Index functions returning s8/u8 to establish a project-wide signedness convention for indices.
